overviews.title: 국제대학원 학과 안내
overviews.content:
# 국제대학원 학과 안내
## MASTER'S PROGRAM

## Introduction

GSIS offers a two-year Master’s program in International Studies. The curriculum is based on five major divisions of study(from 2022 Spring): International Commerce, International Cooperation, International Area Studies, Korean Studies and International Development. Courses are mainly conducted in English, with some selective classes in Korean, Japanese, and Chinese, depending on the major. The academics at GSIS is divided into core curriculum and electives, where the core curriculum helps to develop deep expertise in the major program while electives aim to broaden the students’ perspectives. Most courses are seminar-based, encouraging lively in class discussions to promote active learning. Students are granted a Master of International Studies with a major field of study specified when graduating.

## Degree Requirements

A minimum of 30 credits and submission of thesis

## Major divisions of study

## International Commerce

The curriculum of international commerce at GSIS is diverse. International Commerce students are granted opportunities to delve in to the world of international trade and finance, international business and investment, as well as international trade law and negotiations. The International Commerce major offers an innovative and specialized curriculum to train students into international commerce experts, by providing students with a better understanding of the current socio-political and economic changes through case studies, seminars by experts, and simulation classes.

COURSES

## International Cooperation

A number of interdisciplinary courses on politics, international security, international political economy, international law and organization, and the diversity of culture are provided to students majoring in International Cooperation. The curriculum aims to shed light on the dynamic changes of the international environment so that students can learn to apply the knowledge and promote bilateral and multilateral cooperation.

## International Development

International Development major aims to foster professionals in the field of international development who are qualified with global-level academic expertise with understanding of Korean perspectives. International development major offers diverse theoretical and practical lectures related to international development, economic growth, social development, development policy.

## International Area Studies

International Area Studies trains students to become area specialists who have a comprehensive understanding of specific regions of the world (Asia, America, and Europe), equipping future leaders with extensive knowledge of regional societies. Diverse courses on politics, economics, society, culture, history, geography, the arts and languages are offered.

## Korean Studies

Korean Studies trains students into research specialists on Korea with a comparative and interdisciplinary perspective. Its curriculum, consisting of various courses on Korean literature, history, politics, international relations, anthropology, sociology, economics, and culture, is enhanced by additional practical programs, including field trips and internship opportunities. Korean Studies courses are conducted either in English or Korean.

## Development Cooperation Policy

The Development Cooperation Policy major is offered by GSIS in conjunction with Korea International Cooperation Agency (KOICA), an organization dedicated to fostering the development of human resources in developing countries. Among government officials, employees in public sectors or state institute researches from various countries, twenty students are selected each year to participate in this program with the nomination of their governments, with the aim to create future international experts who will meet the needs of and contribute to the socio-economic advancement of developing countries. The Development Cooperation Policy courses are comprehensive and practical, centering on development policy issues and covering economics, law, and politics.

## * For more detailed information, please refer to the Academic Requirements

overviews.title: 국제대학원 인사말
overviews.content:
# 국제대학원 인사말
## DEAN'S MESSAGE

## GSIS SNU provides

## A truly exceptional research and educational environment.

Since its founding in 1997, the Graduate School of International Studies (GSIS) at Seoul National University has grown into a premier academic and professional institution in South Korea, dedicated to educating and shaping the global leaders of the 21st century. With a diverse student body of 270 master’s and doctoral students from all around the world, we are committed to preparing our graduates to assume critical roles in government, non-governmental organizations, international organizations, multinational corporations, the media, as well as in think tanks and academia in Korea and beyond.

Our curriculum is founded on a multidisciplinary approach that integrates theory with practice, ensuring that our students are equipped with the analytical tools and practical skills necessary to confront the complex global challenges of our time—whether it be geopolitical rivalry, economic security, sustainable development, climate change, or the ongoing transformations with new technologies like AI.

As South Korea emerges as a middle power on the global stage with growing economic, technological, military, and cultural influence, GSIS is positioned at the heart of this dynamic environment, providing unparalleled opportunities for students to engage with these trends both academically and professionally.

GSIS is home to some of Korea’s most distinguished faculty members, specializing in international commerce, international cooperation, international area studies, international development, and Korean studies. Their expertise and dedication create an exceptional research and educational environment where innovative ideas thrive, and intellectual inquiry flourishes. Our faculty’s strength ensures that GSIS remains a hub of knowledge, mutual understanding, and the exchange of ideas on the most critical issues facing the Indo-Pacific and the world.

At GSIS, we are driven by a clear mission: to prepare our students to be global citizens capable of addressing the multifaceted challenges that define the 21st century. We aim not only to deepen our students’ understanding of global affairs but also to provide them with the practical experiences and insights necessary to lead effectively in an increasingly interconnected world.

As dean, I am excited for what lies ahead as we continue to build on GSIS’s proud tradition of academic excellence and global impact. Together, we will ensure that our institution remains at the forefront of international education and academic research for years to come.

## Sheen, Seong-Ho

## Dean, Seoul National University Graduate School of International Studies
## 출처
- 외부: https://gsis.snu.ac.kr/deans-message/

overviews.title: 국제대학원 비전
overviews.content:
# 국제대학원 비전
## WHY GSIS

#### Founded in 1997 as the Institute of International and Area Studies,

## The Graduate School of International Studies (GSIS)

#### has evolved to become the premier graduate school of international studies in Korea.

## Cultivating Connected Intellectuals

Since its foundation, GSIS alumni have gone on to become professionals in academia, as well as in public and private sectors. True to the motto, “think globally, act regionally,” GSIS programs aim to cultivate its students to connect with the world, both regionally and globally, through its integrated multi-disciplinary approach that encompasses both theory and practice.

## Truly International

300 currently enrolled students,50% of international students,55 countries represented in the student bod

The student body at GSIS boasts of highly qualified graduate students from around the globe, including the Americas, the Asia-Pacific, Europe, and Africa. This uniquely international and multicultural environment encourages students to widen their perspectives beyond national and cultural borders.

## World Class Standards in Research and Education

### Research Institute

GSIS has Institute of International Affairs, Institute for Japanese Studies, and growing centers that specialize in international and regional studies to facilitate further in-depth research within GSIS and beyond.

### International Studies Library

GSIS is one of the few graduate schools with its own specialized library. The International Studies Library (ISL) The ISL was designated as Korea’s “EUI (EU Information Center)” by the EU commission. It is stocked with more than 60,000 items.

### Programs

Master's and Doctoral Programs, International Programs including CAMPUS Asia program, which grants three Master’s Degrees course in SNU-GSIS, the School of International Studies at Peking University, and the Graduate School of Public Policy at the University of Tokyo.

### Ranked among the Best

Seoul National University, where GSIS is one of the many professional graduate schools, has been the academic pinnacle of Korea since its foundation. The university is consistently ranked among the top institutions worldwide for prestigious research and education.

## GSIS History

1989

1997

2003

#### Center for Area Studies

In February of 1989, Seoul National University (SNU) established the Center for Area Studies to foster research on region-specific issues and to respond to the increasing demand for global perspectives within Korea

#### School of International and Area Studies (SIAS)

In 1997, the Center for Area Studies expanded to become the School of International and Area Studies (SIAS). SIAS was launched as a joint course program, offering students an array of masters' degrees including economics, business administration, political science, art and law. At the same time, trade negotiations, international cooperation, area studies, and Korean studies were firmly established as major fields in SIAS.

#### Graduate School of International Studies (GSIS)

On 1 March, 2003, the school expanded once again to become the Graduate School of International Studies (GSIS), the premier graduate school of international affairs and area studies in Korea. This history of institutional development reflects SNU's continual efforts to meet the demands of a dynamic world. Consistent with this mission, GSIS SNU has established itself as an institution that continues to meet the challenges of today, training the international experts of tomorrow.

## GSIS Vision

GSIS SNU educates global leaders for an increasingly complex global society. As both a professional and academic institution, it seeks to prepare its students to assume leading roles in government and non-government organizations, international organizations, multinational corporations, the media, as well as think-tanks and universities. The curriculum is based on an integrated, multidisciplinary approach, which connects theory with practice.

With the most prominent faculty in Korea specializing in international commerce, international cooperation, international area studies, International Development and Korean studies, GSIS SNU provides a truly exceptional research and educational environment.

The student body is composed of highly qualified graduate students from all around the world. As a source of knowledge and skills, GSIS SNU will continue to promote mutual understanding, peace, and prosperity, both regionally and globally.
